name: MRINJOY Partners
tagline: Redesigning the digital face of an IP law firm
description: A full redesign of an intellectual property law firm's website.
problem: Law firm websites are notoriously dated, dense, and difficult to navigate. MRINJOY Partners needed a digital presence reflecting their modern, authoritative approach to IP law.
approach: We initiated a complete content audit, reducing legalese and structuring the architecture around core practice areas. The design leans heavily on an editorial, magazine-like aesthetic.
designDecisions: Developed the 'Ink & Ivory' color system to convey trust and prestige. Used expansive typography (Playfair Display) to mimic physical legal documents.
technicalArchitecture: A React frontend powered by Vite, with Framer Motion handling subtle page transitions that emulate turning pages in a portfolio.
challenges: Migrating decades of legal publications and standardizing the CMS schema without losing legacy SEO value.
outcome: Currently in development, but initial user testing shows a 40% increase in time-on-page and significantly easier navigation.
keyLearnings: Content architecture is the design. You cannot design a premium layout around unstructured, dense text.

name: Labthread
tagline: A research collaboration and thread-tracking platform
description: Built for academic labs — a lightweight space to track research threads.
problem: Academic research is often scattered across endless email chains, Slack channels, and undocumented lab notebooks, making it impossible to trace the origin of an idea.
approach: Labthread introduces 'threads' as first-class citizens. Everything is an immutable log attached to a specific hypothesis, allowing collaborators to append data sequentially.
designDecisions: The UI mimics a command-line interface meets modern messaging app. Extremely high data density, monospaced fonts for data logs, and minimal distraction.
technicalArchitecture: A React SPA communicating with a Node.js backend. Websockets are used for real-time updates when collaborators append to a thread.
challenges: Designing a permissions model flexible enough for principal investigators, post-docs, and external collaborators without complex setup.
outcome: In closed beta with three academic labs, iterating based on real-world usage patterns.
keyLearnings: Scientists don't want another social network; they want a structured, search-optimized database for their thoughts.
